title: arenaria congesta var. suffrutescens
intro: Arenaria congesta var. suffrutescens, also known as dwarf sandwort, is a low-growing, succulent plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Arenaria congesta var. suffrutescens include its potential to help reduce blood pressure, alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use as a medicinal plant is not as widely recognized or documented as some other species within the same family.
